sources_summary: Compiled from CENTCOM statements, CSIS/RUSI/Payne Institute analysis, USNI Fleet Tracker, satellite imagery, and open-source media reporting through April 7, 2026.

tomahawk_cruise_missiles.notes: ~850 expended from estimated 4000 pre-war inventory; Day 4 transition to JDAMs sharply reduced expenditure rate.

gbu57_massive_ordnance_penetrator.notes: 14 used in June 2025 Midnight Hammer; possible additional use at Taleghan 2 unconfirmed; Boeing replenishment contract covers delivery 2028-2030.

sm3_sm6_naval_interceptors.notes: ~80 expended in June 2025 Twelve-Day War plus ~180 combined naval interceptors in Epic Fury opening phase; exact SM-3 breakout classified.

thaad_interceptors.notes: 100-150 expended in June 2025 plus ~40 in Epic Fury; production ~100-130/year; 2-4 AN/TPY-2 radars destroyed rendering some batteries non-operational.

patriot_pac3_mse_interceptors.notes: ~90 fired by US in opening phase; UAE alone used ~200; production surge from 500 to 2000/year authorized but not yet flowing.

carrier_strike_groups_centcom.notes: Lincoln operational in Arabian Sea; Ford departing after fire repair; Bush en route from Norfolk to relieve Ford.

ballistic_missiles.notes: ~480 fired Day 1, rate collapsed 92% by Day 10; IDF estimates 70% of launchers disabled though US intelligence assesses only ~50% destroyed.

shahed_owa_drones.notes: ~2000+ expended in first 4 days; pre-war inventory estimates vary 3x; dispersed production makes depletion extremely difficult to verify.

ballistic_missile_launchers.notes: IDF estimates 290 of 410-440 launchers destroyed by Day 16; US intelligence more conservative at ~50% destroyed; divergence is significant.

naval_vessels.notes: 100+ vessels destroyed or disabled per CENTCOM by mid-March from ~250-300 pre-war fleet; IRGCN commander Tangsiri killed March 26.

naval_mines.notes: Fewer than 10 mines reportedly deployed as of mid-March despite 6000 pre-war stockpile; 16+ minelayers destroyed by CENTCOM.

air_defense_systems.notes: US claimed air superiority by Day 6 but F-15E shootdown April 3 confirms residual Bavar-373 or S-300 capability; no comprehensive battery count available.

arrow_interceptors.notes: RUSI estimates 81.33% depleted by Day 16; IDF denies critical shortage; US moving THAAD from South Korea as emergency supplement.

f35i_adir_aircraft.notes: No confirmed losses in Epic Fury; Iran's June 2025 claims of 3 kills were denied and unsubstantiated; 2600+ sorties conducted across IAF fleet.

standoff_precision_munitions.notes: 6500+ munitions expended in first 10 days of Roaring Lion alone; already partially depleted from June 2025 and Gaza operations pre-war.

tomahawk_cruise_missiles.notes: Peak rate ~130-160/day in first 72 hours across 13 destroyers and submarines.
Day 4 transition to stand-in JDAM strikes reduced Tomahawk expenditure to ~20-40/day for conservation.

thaad_interceptors.notes: Heavy demand during peak Iranian ballistic missile launches of ~480/day.
Rate declined sharply as Iranian launch tempo dropped 92% by Day 10.

sm3_sm6_naval_interceptors.notes: Peak intercept demand during Iranian ballistic missile salvo phase of ~480 launches/day.
Reduced rate as Iranian missile launches collapsed; residual demand from ongoing lower-tempo strikes.

ballistic_missiles.notes: Peak launch rate Day 1 of ~480; CENTCOM confirmed 500+ in first 4 days total.
Rate collapsed 92% by Day 10 due to launcher destruction, C2 degradation, and possible stockpile conservation.

shahed_owa_drones.notes: Peak rate ~720 Day 1; 2000+ total in first 4 days per CENTCOM.
Sustained rate 50-100/day from mid-March; dispersed production may partially offset losses.

ballistic_missile_launchers.notes: IDF assessed 300 launchers destroyed by Day 4 from a pre-war base of 410-440.
Slower destruction rate as high-value mobile launchers dispersed and CENTCOM shifted to precision stand-in strikes.

arrow_interceptors.notes: RUSI estimated 81.33% depletion by Day 16 implying ~5% of pre-war stock consumed per day at peak Iranian ballistic threat tempo.
Reduced but ongoing depletion as Iranian launches sustained at 15-30 ballistic missiles/day at all targets.

infrastructure_status.name: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
infrastructure_status.notes: Struck by 6 GBU-57 MOPs in June 2025 and again in February 2026; assessed inoperable with no IAEA access.

infrastructure_status.name: Natanz Fuel Enrichment Plant
infrastructure_status.notes: Above-ground facilities destroyed; underground halls degraded with possible centrifuge damage from power loss; no IAEA access.

infrastructure_status.name: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant Unit 1
infrastructure_status.notes: Precautionary shutdown by Rosatom; struck four times with near-misses; Russian staff evacuating; no radiation release confirmed.

infrastructure_status.name: Kharg Island Oil Export Terminal
infrastructure_status.notes: Oil terminal physically intact after March 13 raid targeting military sites only; functionally useless due to Hormuz closure.

infrastructure_status.name: South Pars Gas Field
infrastructure_status.notes: Israeli strikes caused partial degradation; largely intact but surrounding infrastructure damage cascades into system-wide reduction.

infrastructure_status.name: Ras Laffan LNG Complex
infrastructure_status.notes: Strike caused 17% reduction in Qatar LNG capacity; fires contained; repairs estimated at 3–5 years; global LNG supply reduced 20%.

infrastructure_status.name: Al Udeid Air Base
infrastructure_status.notes: AN/FPS-132 early warning radar ($1.1B) destroyed; SATCOM infrastructure destroyed; operationally active but with critical sensor gaps.

infrastructure_status.name: Muwaffaq Salti Air Base
infrastructure_status.notes: AN/TPY-2 THAAD radar confirmed destroyed by Pentagon; base operational but BMD capability critically compromised.

infrastructure_status.name: Ain al-Assad Air Base
infrastructure_status.notes: Ballistic missile strike killed 3 US service members (first KIA of conflict) and wounded 12; base remains operational.

infrastructure_status.name: ADNOC Habshan Gas Processing Complex
infrastructure_status.notes: Twice shut down by interceptor debris igniting fires (March 19 and April 3); significant damage assessed; one Egyptian national killed April 3.

infrastructure_status.name: Bandar Abbas Naval Port and Refinery
infrastructure_status.notes: IRNS Makran ablaze at pier; naval fuel facilities damaged; IRGC Navy commander Tangsiri killed here March 26 by Israeli airstrike.

infrastructure_status.name: Tehran–Karaj Power Grid (Alborz Province)
infrastructure_status.notes: Strikes on Alborz province substations caused partial Tehran blackouts; grid partially restored but stability compromised; catastrophic failure threatened by Trump's April 7 deadline.

infrastructure_status.name: Tehran–Karaj B1 Bridge
infrastructure_status.notes: Destroyed by US airstrike; identified as key missile supply route to western Iran; Trump posted video of collapse.

infrastructure_status.name: Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery
infrastructure_status.notes: Kuwait's largest refinery struck by drone attacks April 3 setting units on fire; Kuwait Petroleum Corporation reported significant operational facility damage April 5.

infrastructure_status.name: Saudi Arabia East-West Pipeline (Petroline)
infrastructure_status.notes: Surged to full 7 million bpd capacity by end of March from 770,000 bpd pre-war baseline; single most important Hormuz bypass route globally.

global_variable_timeline.notes: Pre-war baseline; Omani FM announced diplomatic breakthrough day before strikes began.
Operation Epic Fury launched; Hormuz shipping halted as IRGC issued closure warnings and attacks on vessels began.
Iran formally declared Strait closed; IRGC began merchant vessel attacks; 2,000+ ships stranded in Persian Gulf.
Brent surpassed $100/bbl for first time in four years; IEA described supply loss as exceeding 1973 and 1979 crises combined.
IEA unanimously approved 400 million barrel emergency release (largest in agency history); price impact limited without Hormuz reopening.
Brent briefly dipped below $100 after Trump signaled military wind-down in two to three weeks; Ras Laffan damage locked in LNG shortfall.
Trump national address warning further aggression sent WTI surging 11%; physical spot touched $141.36 briefly; B1 bridge destroyed.
Trump 8pm ET deadline for Hormuz reopening or power grid/bridge strikes; 53 weekly transits recorded under Iran's selective passage system, still down 90%+ from normal.

political_indicators.us_congressional_status: War Powers Resolutions failed in both chambers (47-53 Senate, 212-219 House); no AUMF voted on; $200B supplemental proposed but facing resistance from both parties.
political_indicators.iran_domestic_status: Mojtaba Khamenei installed as Supreme Leader March 9 under heavy IRGC pressure; no video or audio released; state apparatus effectively IRGC-controlled; Iran formally rejected US ceasefire framework and issued 10 counter-demands via Pakistani intermediaries.
political_indicators.nato_cohesion: No NATO member providing direct military support; Spain denied US base access entirely; seven allies signed Hormuz political statement with no naval commitments; Trump called alliance a 'paper tiger' and threatened its future.
